qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page provides details of completed events which are part of the infrastructure renewal activities.

qa_pairs.question: Which compute clusters were fully decommissioned by September 2025 as part of infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara, Mist, and Graham were fully decommissioned by September 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the initial capacity reduction for the Niagara and Mist clusters starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara operated at approximately 50% capacity, and Mist at approximately 35% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the initial capacity reductions on Niagara and Mist in early 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: These reductions supported ongoing system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: When was Trillium Open OnDemand launched?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium Open OnDemand was launched on September 11, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the operational status of the Graham compute cluster from February 25, 2025, until its retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham operated at approximately 25% capacity while data migration and system transition work were underway.

qa_pairs.question: Did Graham Cloud remain operational during the Graham cluster's reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Graham Cloud remained fully operational throughout the reduction period.

qa_pairs.question: What service was unavailable on Béluga, Narval, and Juno from July 15 to August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The tape storage system behind the TSM service, including backups and migrated /nearline data, was unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the impact on file backup and restore services during the tape storage migration in July-August 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: File backup and restore services were unavailable, and data created or modified between July 15 and August 11 could not be recovered.

qa_pairs.question: Were compute nodes or cloud instances affected during the tape storage outage from July 15 to August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, other storage systems, compute nodes on all clusters, and Juno Cloud instances remained fully operational.

qa_pairs.question: Why did the Fir cluster experience a temporary reduction in compute nodes from August 25 to September 5, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The reduction was to support the commissioning of new cooling equipment.

qa_pairs.question: What was the impact on Fir users during the August-September 2025 reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Fewer jobs ran concurrently, and jobs often started more slowly with longer wait times.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the Rorqual compute cluster's full unavailability on September 3, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: It was fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance.

qa_pairs.question: What caused the extended full data centre shutdown for Graham from December 7, 2024, to February 24, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Ongoing renovations required a complete data centre shutdown, making all Graham cluster services, storage, and cloud services entirely unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the purpose of the Cedar cluster outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage supported critical data-centre power and cooling upgrades for new system installation.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs running on the Cedar cluster during its March 31 to April 2, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Any jobs running at the time were terminated and had to be re-submitted once the cluster came back online.

qa_pairs.question: Did Cedar Cloud remain operational during the March 2025 Cedar outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar Cloud remained operational during this period.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the SciNet Datacenter outage on November 7-8, 2024, affecting multiple systems like Niagara and Mist?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was necessary for installing new electrical equipment (UPS) as part of a systems refresh.

qa_pairs.question: What user action was recommended for the Cedar /scratch filesystem before the January 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were asked to move any important data immediately to their /project, /nearline, or /home directory.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the temporary reduction of Béluga and Narval compute nodes from January 13 to February 14, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Performance and stability tests on Rorqual required the shutdown of compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: During the temporary reduction of Béluga and Narval (Jan 13 - Feb 14, 2025), was storage accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, login nodes and data access remained operational, and Béluga Storage was mounted to Narval.

qa_pairs.question: Were Cedar login nodes, storage, and cloud services affected by the compute node unavailability on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Cedar login nodes, storage, and cloud services remained operational and unaffected.
